name: Griñón, Móstoles, Brunete, Navalcarnero – circular route
name_original: Griñón, Móstoles, Brunete, Navalcarnero – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Griñón, located in the Community of Madrid, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The area features a mix of flatter stretches and ascents, including notable climbs, providing varied experiences. The terrain includes riverside groves along the Guadarrama River and accessible green spaces, making it suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Griñón The most popular road cycling route is Griñón, Móstoles, Brunete, Navalcarnero – circular route, a 48.7 miles (78.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering a comprehensive tour of the local area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Plaza de Segovia – Navalcarnero Main Square loop from Humanes, a moderate 54.2 miles (87.3 km) path. This route features a mix of open roads and passes through urban centers, providing varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the XXL Bicycle Sculpture – Plaza de Segovia loop from Humanes, a 44.4 miles (71.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain and local landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Griñón is defined by varied terrain, including plains, gentle ascents, and routes near the Guadarrama River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Griñón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 6,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Griñón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Griñón?
A: Griñón offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 70 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Griñón?
A: The terrain around Griñón is quite diverse, featuring a mix of flatter stretches, gentle ascents, and more challenging climbs. You'll find routes that traverse plains, follow riverside groves along the Guadarrama River, and include notable ascents like the 'Mortirolo Climb of Batres'.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Griñón?
A: Yes, for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the area around Griñón includes specific climbs such as the 'Mortirolo Climb of Batres'. This ascent is known among local riders for its average gradient and elevation gain, providing a good test of endurance.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Griñón?
A: While road cycling around Griñón, you can encounter several natural features and landmarks. Highlights include the beautiful riverside groves along the Guadarrama River near Batres Castle. Further afield, you might explore the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1585228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Nelson Mandela Park&lt;/a&gt; or ride through the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2957869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Móstoles&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1393652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Teja Spring and the Hermitage of San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any lakes or forests accessible by road bike near Griñón?
A: Yes, the region offers access to beautiful natural areas. You can cycle towards the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1585228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Nelson Mandela Park&lt;/a&gt;, which features calm waters and various paths. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2957869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Móstoles&lt;/a&gt; provides an excellent natural escape with multiple trails that can be enjoyed throughout the year.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Griñón?
A: Absolutely. Many of the road cycling routes around Griñón are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977179642/grinon-mostoles-brunete-navalcarnero-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griñón, Móstoles, Brunete, Navalcarnero – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the local area.

Q: Can I find easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Griñón?
A: Yes, Griñón has options for beginners. Out of the nearly 70 routes, 23 are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to road cycling in the area. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, perfect for those new to the sport.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling tours from Griñón?
A: For those looking for longer rides, Griñón offers several routes that cover significant distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32054158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Segovia – Navalcarnero Main Square loop from Humanes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 54.2 miles (87.3 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977179642/grinon-mostoles-brunete-navalcarnero-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griñón, Móstoles, Brunete, Navalcarnero – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is 48.7 miles (78.4 km).

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Griñón?
A: The road cycling routes in Griñón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both open roads and scenic natural areas, providing a diverse and enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Griñón?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly highlighted, the presence of local parks like Jardines del Alba and Fuente de la Salud, along with accessible green spaces and easy routes, suggests that some of the less challenging paths could be suitable for families. It's advisable to check the difficulty and length of easy routes for suitability.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Griñón?
A: The broader Madrid region, where Griñón is located, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild, but it's always best to check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Griñón?
A: While specific parking locations for cyclists are not detailed, as a town within the Community of Madrid, Griñón would have general public parking facilities. It is recommended to check local maps or municipal websites for designated parking areas near potential route starting points.
tips: Downhill section that allows you to go fast without losing the landscape of the Guadarrama valley

In Navalcarnero you can go from square to square, enjoying a well-deserved rest. There are squares with a long history, such as Plaza de Segovia, and recently created squares such as D. Francisco Sandoval, Plaza V Centenario, Plaza de la Virgen de la Cueva, etc.
A purple gorilla! Something fun along our route. You can not lose this.
Cauliflower is the product that makes the town famous. In March they have the Cauliflower Festival in which specimens are distributed to visitors and all possible varieties are tasted.
